concise_definition: Early offense : Action that occurs when a team in possession of the basketball attempts to score quickly before the opposing team is fully set and prepared to limit or prevent scoring opportunities.

sections.heading: What is early offense in basketball?
sections.text: Early offense comprises action that occurs when a team in possession of the basketball attempts to score quickly before the opposing team is fully set and prepared to limit or prevent scoring opportunities.

sections.heading: What is an example of an early offense play featuring a backdoor cut?
sections.text:
This is an example of an early offense play that features abackdoor cut, which could result in a quick scoring opportunity at the rim.

What’s more, generally speaking, it can be assumed that the opposing team missed afield goalattempt, prior to the initiation of earlytransition offense, unless stated otherwise.

[Image Alt Text: An example of an early offense play featuring a backdoor cut.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of an early offense play featuring a backdoor cut.]

To start, 5, thecenter, grabs the defensive rebound near the basket. As that happens, 1, thepoint guard, performs av-cutto get open and receive the ball from 5, indicated by the dotted arrow.

Next, 1 begins dribbling towards thefrontcourt, represented by the wavy arrow. While that occurs, theshooting guard, 2, and thesmall forward, 3 run towards the right side and left sidecorners, respectively, which are also shown via the solid arrows.

Thepower forward, 4, also runs towards the leftslotarea while 5 runs into the frontcourt near the right slot to set adrag screen. Afterwards, 1 uses that screen and dribbles toward the left slot area while 5 pops to the top.

Following that, 5 dribbles at 2 as if to initiate adribble handoff. Furthermore, 2 briefly steps toward 5 as if to participate in that action.

However, 2 then swiftly backdoor cuts to the basket, receives the ball from 5, preferably via abounce pass, and scores at the rim by way of alayup.

Related:Best Basketball Plays: Backdoor Set Plays That Work – YouTube

sections.heading: What is an example of a early offense play featuring a stagger screen?
sections.text:
This is an example of an early offense play that looks to create a possible scoring opportunity from three-point range near the corner via astagger screen.

[Image Alt Text: An example of an early offense play featuring an offensive player cutting to the corner via a stagger screen.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of an early offense play featuring an offensive player cutting to the corner via a stagger screen.]

To begin, 4 collects the defensive rebound and quickly passes the ball, which is received by 1. After that, 1 dribbles into the frontcourt while 2 and 3 initially sprint towards thewingareas.

Also, as that happens, 4 sprints toward the right slot area to set a drag screen. Furthermore, 3 and 5 sprint toward the basket to set a stagger screen.

1 utilizes the drag screen and as that occurs, 2 runs the baseline to the left side corner via the stagger screen action.

Following that, 2 receives possession of the ball from 1 and takes the open three-point shot.

sections.heading: What is an example of an early offense play featuring loop action?
sections.text:
This is an example of an early offense play that utilizes loop action followed by astagger screento create a scoring opportunity from the perimeter.

[Image Alt Text: An example of an early offense play that showcases loop action resulting in a scoring opportunity.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of an early offense play that showcases loop action resulting in a scoring opportunity.]

To start, 4 collects the rebound and as that happens, 1 uses a small v-cut to get open. Afterwards, 1 receives the ball from 4 and begins dribbling towards the right side wing.

As that happens, 2 sprints towards the strong side corner while 3 sprints to the weak side corner. Furthermore, 4 and 5 also run down the court to set a stagger screen.

Next, as 1 continues toward the right side wing, 2 basically zipper cuts back toward the top via the stagger screen, which completes the loop action.

Following that, 2 receives possession of the ball from 1 and takes the open three-point shot.

sections.heading: What is an example of an early offense play featuring a wide pin down screen?
sections.text:
This is an example of an early offense play that features a wide pin down screen andcurl cutto create a scoring opportunity near the basket.

[Image Alt Text: An example of an early offense play that showcases a wide pin down screen and curl cut that could result in a quick scoring opportunity at the basket.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of an early offense play that showcases a wide pin down screen and curl cut that could result in a quick scoring opportunity at the basket.]

To begin, 5 grabs possession of the ball via a defensive rebound. From there, 1 gets open by way of a v-cut and receives the ball from 5.

Next, 1 begins to dribble towards the frontcourt while 2 and 3 sprint to the right side wing and left side corner, respectively. 5 also trails the play.

Furthermore, 4 initially runs towards the frontcourt before cutting towards the left side corner. As that occurs, 2 receives the ball from 1 via the pitch ahead pass.

Following that, 4 sets a wide pin down screen near the left corner. In addition, 3 uses that screen and gets open by way of a curl cut to the basket.

Afterwards, 3 receives the ball from 2 and has an opportunity to score around the basket via a layup.
